name: Progress Sitefinity CMS and DXP API
what_it_does: Enterprise CMS and Digital Experience Platform with REST and OData API — enabling .NET development teams to create and manage content, personalize experiences, run A/B tests, and deliver headless content, with native .NET/ASP.NET integration, drag-and-drop page builder, marketing automation, and deep integration with Progress Telerik UI components and KendoUI.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Sitefinity REST Web Services API for Next.js or React frontends
Managing content types and pages via Sitefinity OData API for content migration and automation
Personalizing website experiences via Sitefinity Personalization API for customer segment targeting
Integrating Sitefinity with Salesforce and marketing tools via API for CRM data synchronization
Running A/B tests and analyzing results via Sitefinity Testing API for conversion optimization
not_for: Organizations without .NET development expertise (Sitefinity requires .NET for on-premises)
Small and mid-market teams without DXP budget
Open-source-first organizations (Sitefinity is commercial, not open source)
best_when: A .NET-centric organization using Progress/Telerik developer tools needs a DXP that integrates natively with their .NET stack — especially where Telerik/KendoUI component integration and .NET familiarity are priorities.
avoid_when: You don't use .NET, need open-source CMS, prefer SaaS-native headless CMS, or are not already in the Progress/Telerik ecosystem.

auth.notes: OAuth2 authorization code and client credentials flows. Bearer token auth. Role-based access via Sitefinity security framework. Webhook for content publish and user events. REST Web Services API + OData API for headless content delivery. .NET SDK for server-side integration. JavaScript SDK for frontend. SAML/Claims-based auth for enterprise SSO. Progress Telerik ecosystem integration.

pricing.paid_tiers: Business from $18,000/year; Enterprise from $35,000/year; contact Progress sales
pricing.notes: Boston company (Progress Software, NASDAQ: PRGS). Founded 1981. Sitefinity acquired 2009. $580M+ revenue. Progress is a .NET/Java middleware company (OpenEdge, DataDirect, Telerik, Kendo). Telerik and KendoUI are sister products — developer tools integration. 3,000+ Sitefinity customers. Used by pharmaceutical, financial services, higher education sectors. Strong in US mid-market and Telerik customer base. Progress One Platform for unified .NET developer experience. Marketing automation via Marketo/Salesforce integration.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST + OData API for headless delivery. OAuth2 auth. Good .NET-focused documentation. Webhook for content events. .NET developer expertise required. OData query support provides flexible content querying. SAML/Claims-based SSO. Progress NASDAQ-listed with enterprise security standards.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. OData queries idempotent. Page reads idempotent by URL or ID. Content mutations use content ID for idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: .NET developer required for on-premises deployment and customization
OData API in addition to REST — two query patterns to understand
Progress Telerik ecosystem lock-in — best value when already using Telerik/KendoUI
On-premises deployment requires Windows Server + IIS or .NET-compatible Linux

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Self-hosted and US/EU cloud data residency. OAuth2 auth. NASDAQ-listed Progress with enterprise security standards. SAML/Claims-based SSO for enterprise identity. HIPAA BAA for healthcare. Self-hosted gives full data control. Sitefinity permissions are role-based — configure carefully for headless API access. Content may include personalization data — GDPR compliance required.
